title: phacelia glandulosa subsp. splendens
intro: Phacelia glandulosa subsp. splendens, a species of flowering plant in the borage family, is a fragrant shrub native to western North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's medicinal properties are said to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. Phacelia glandulosa subsp. splendens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or topical applications, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional.
